stages.heading: Stardate 2400.0 (Introduction)
commentary: Welcome to Doomsday Machines meet a Talon board. Although not an official Space Empires 4X scenario, we shake things up by taking advantage of the wider width; at the expense of a slightly reduced height. This also allows us to play around with Victory Points in a solo setting, which was made officially available exclusively as cooperative scenarios (2-4 players) and also for Alien Empires. Although we have a reduced height, All Good Things introduced a hidden Galactic Capitol rule in some layouts. This objective is placed similarly to the solo map; any adjacent hex beyond row I.

Since we are experimenting relatively deep into uncharted territory, we will conduct this scenario as a campaign. In order to make it a thorough process, we must playtest as many difficulty settings as possible; which means we must start with Easy.

stages.heading: Stardate 2400.3
commentary: Hopefully this process works out. We got a lot of ground to cover.

stages.heading: Stardate 2400.6
commentary: ... yet, this is only the first step of a potentially long process.

stages.heading: Stardate 2400.9
commentary: Still, this is one of the reasons why we dubbed this a campaign.

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.0
commentary: Unlike the non-VP scenarios, Doomsday Machines in the VP scenarios gain some additional abilities. Most notably, they actually uncover any unexplored hexes they reach. They also gain 2 VP for each planet they destroy. There are also two types of DMs. Extermination DMs usually go straight for a homeworld. Exploration DMs tend to seek out planets. Behavior varies wildly from player count.

Since we are treating this as if this was a 2-player scenario, exploration DMs will try to avoid the galactic capitol. Instead, that is where the different colored markers below come into play. Any extermination DMs that spawn on a white marker will seek out the galactic capitol. Colored markers the homeworld of matching color. Notice that 2 hexes have 2 markers each (different colors), this is used to further split how a d10 should be handled whenever an extermination DM enters the board.
{dmTable}

Since the DMs have new abilities that are not available in non-VP scenarios, we can not really play defensively anymore. Especially since each NPA planet we colonize or capture deducts 2 VP from the DM team.
Still, we should expand our economy before we go very far into technology. Since buying and applying Exploration 1 now (18 total) would spoil 3 Colony Ships, we instead buy 4 colony ships (-32).

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.2
commentary: Not much else to say just yet...

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.5
commentary: ... while the home systems are being uncovered.

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.8
commentary: ... and while we still have not yet seen the black hole.

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.0
commentary: Now that we have more colony ships in circulation, we can afford to spend our next money on other matters. We will start with Exploration 1 (-15) and Ship Size 2 (-10).

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.1
commentary: Some of the money that we had leftover was set aside to upgrade our Flagship (-3). We also finally expose our home system black hole. The Scout that just ran into it rolled a 4, escaping its grasp decently.

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.4
commentary: Home Systems can be quite... trollish. Equipping our Flagship with Exploration was not a total waste though; we can still use it for deep space.

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.7
commentary: Already we have found the galactic capitol. This makes it easier to determine where to defend, as need be.

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.0
commentary: More than half of our grace period is gone. We bought Attack 1 (-20), Defense 1 (-20), and 1 Colony Ship (-8).

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.3
commentary: Perhaps this is finally our chance to try our hand at Deep Space Planet Attributes. NPAs themselves are not only enabled, but also required.
For these planets, we drew Minor Technology and Wealthy. We then draw the appropriate ships for each planet, including some of the new toys the NPA get to play with.
Now that preparations are all done, Aries has rolled 6/3,4,8 (2 hits ≤5). Cygni has rolled 2,4 (2 hits ≤5). These planets have eliminated our scouts.

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.6
commentary: Whereas barren planets claim our first two scouts, a danger marker claims the last...

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.9
commentary: Still, learning 2 barren planets next to our home systems is useful information.

stages.heading: Stardate 2404.0
commentary: The good news about these recent deep space losses is that our maintenance has zero-ed out... for now. It will be just a bit longer before we are ready to commit to a fleet. We will buy Ship Size 3 (-15) and Attack 2 (-30) while this is still incomplete, followed by an extra Ship Yard (-6).

stages.heading: Stardate 2405.0
commentary: There was hardly any meaningful stages now that only our Flagship and Miner are doing anything meaningful (yet), so we have opted to cut it straight to the next economic phase.
Anyway, we are almost out of time. We will finish the technology process and buy Defense 2 (-30). This then gives us money to buy 2 Cruisers (-24). We then buy an extra Ship Yard (-6) on top of that.

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.0
commentary: And with the last quickie out of the way, we have ran out of time. The first DM is now on the board! We rolled a 6 to determine its initial position, followed by a 2 for its type (extermination DM ≤6). Considering where it spawned, its destination is the galactic capitol.
{dmTable}

We will go on ahead and buy Ship Yards 2 (-20) to open up options. We now have capacity for another 3 Cruisers (-36). Finally, we throw down a forward Ship Yard at Kronos (I10), intended to cut down on backtracking for our Flagship.

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.3
commentary: Unlike the non-VP scenarios, DMs move first unless we bid 5 CP. As usual where changing is possible, it is sometimes advantageous to allow the antagonist to have the initiative.
Anyhow, now that the action has started ramping up again, we will be going through this process slower.

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.6
commentary: Prior to All Good Things, minerals used to contribute to VP for both DMs and Alien Players. The former now just simply destroy them.

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.9
commentary: We have finished upgrades on our Flagship. This should be the last time we need to do so.

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.0
commentary: The second DM has now appeared on the board. 2,8 causes it to spawn on the far left, as an exploration DM. We buy Ship Yards 3 (-25), followed by 3 more Cruisers (-36).

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.2
commentary: Now that there are two DMs on the board, they are uncovering deep space twice as fast.

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.5
commentary: We are almost prepared to fend off the first DM...

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.8D (The Battle for the Galactic Capitol)
commentary: We are ready to have the first battle of the playthrough! Let's see how well our rolls work out.
1. Round 1: The first DM has rolled a 7 for weakness... large fleets
• Our ships rolled 2,2,7,9,9,10 (2 hits ≤5), reducing the DM's HP to 4
• The DM rolled 3,6,10 against our Cruisers (1 hit ≤4)
• Round 2: Our ships rolled 1,3,3,4,6,7 (4 hits ≤5), finishing off this DM easily!

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.8P
commentary: We took no losses during their turn. We simply consolidate our ships west.

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.0
commentary: Another economic phase, another DM spawn. 2,2 causes them to be far left, only as an extermination DM against our homeworld. We have money to buy another 5 Cruisers (-60).

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.1
commentary: The newest DM has found Deneb, another NPA planet. We drew Builder for this planet, causing them to have 5 NPA ships instead of the usual 4. Since this is the DM's first battle, we will go on ahead and conduct it anyway, despite it seeming to be one-sided.
1. Round 1: The DM rolls a 2 for weakness (fighters)
• Their Alien-A rolls a 10 (≤3 to hit)
• The DM rolls 8,3,9,1 against the Alien-Cs (2 hits ≤7), and a 10 against their Alien-E (≤8 to hit)
• Their Alien-D rolls a 1 (hit ≤2)
• Their Alien-E rolls a 5 (hit ≤5)
• Round 2: Their Alien-A rolls a 4 (≤3 to hit)
• The DM rolls 6,8 against the Alien-E (2 hits ≤8), and 10,2,6 against their Alien-A (2 hits ≤8)
• Their Alien-D rolls a 5 (≤2 to hit)
• Round 3: The DM rolls 2,5,7,8,10 against the Alien-D (4 hits ≤8), eliminating this group and their planet

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.4
commentary: This marks the first time the DMs have gained VP. And of course, the marbles start to snowball... 2 NPA planets, back to back! This one is Desolate, making it extremely easy for the DM to take out 1 ship. They rolled a 4,6,8,10,10 (3 hits ≤8), pulling off exactly that.

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.7
commentary: Things have gotten a little dicier. We now have our first ion storm.

stages.heading: Stardate 2409.0
commentary: We have a small reprieve. Thanks to some dense minerals, we are able to afford another 5 Cruisers (-60).
This is also the first time we perform storm movement. The sole known ion storm has rolled an 8. Since that result matches none of the sides shown, it stays still.

stages.heading: Stardate 2409.3D (The Battle for Bajor)
commentary: The second DM is now ready to battle, having rolled a 3 for weakness (minelayers).
1. Round 1: The DM has the best weapon class this time, so it goes first. It rolled 2,8,10,10 (1 hit ≤5)
• Our 8 Cruisers rolled 5,6,7,7,7,9,9,10,10 (0 hits ≤4)
• Round 2: The DM rolls 1,3,7,8 (2 hits ≤5)
• Our 7 Cruisers rolled 1,1,3,6,8,9,9 (3 hits ≤4), reducing the DM's HP to 4
• Round 3: The DM rolls 1,5,6,10 (2 hits ≤5)
• Our 6 Cruisers rolled 2,3,5,5,6,7 (2 hits ≤4), reducing the DM's HP to 2
• Round 4: The DM rolls 1,5,10,10 (2 hits ≤5)
• Our 5 Cruisers rolled 2,3,4,7,10 (3 hits ≤4), finishing this DM off

stages.heading: Stardate 2409.3P
commentary: We take advantage of our recent victory, and move our surviving Cruisers south-west to perform some quick Exploration.

stages.heading: Stardate 2409.6
commentary: Things have calmed a bit now that there is only one DM in play once more.

stages.heading: Stardate 2409.9
commentary: We now have a single consolidated force ready to defend against the third DM.

stages.heading: Stardate 2410.0
commentary: But; before we can put it to good use, we need to spawn another DM. We rolled 3,8; spawning an exploration DM at C3. We then buy another 4 Cruisers (-48).
For the sole ion storm, we rolled a 6. The cosmic storm tries to move, but the warp point is blocking the way. Thus, it stays once more.

stages.heading: Stardate 2410.2D (The Battle for Sector K5)
commentary: An extermination DM will avoid unnecessary battles... as long as doing so would not delay its ability to reach its destination. Since this condition is false, it moves into our fleet, and battles anyway. Since its weakness was previously known (fighters), we go straight to battle.
1. Round 1: The DM rolls 2,3,3,7,7 against our cruisers (3 hits ≤6)
• Our 15 ships rolled 8/1,1,2,3,5,6,6,7,7,9,10,10,10,10 (4 hits ≤4), reducing the DM's HP to 4
• Round 2: The DM rolls 4,5,7,7,8 against our cruisers (2 hits ≤6)
• Our 14 ships rolled 2/2,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,6,6,7,9,9 (5 hits ≤4), finishing this DM off also

stages.heading: Stardate 2410.2P
commentary: Now it is our turn to move. We have a new target considerably south of us...

stages.heading: Stardate 2410.5
commentary: Once again, the board calms down.

stages.heading: Stardate 2410.8 (The Battle for Abydos)
commentary: Space Pirates are not explicitly forbidden per se; but a DM automatically defeats one when revealing one.
Meanwhile, we remotely explore a NPA planet. For VP-related reasons, we must engage against them to deny the DM progression. They benefit from the Accurate perk.
1. Round 1: Their Alien-A goes first, having rolled a 6 against our cruisers (≤3 to hit)
• Benefitting from Fleet Size Bonus, our ships then rolled 9/1,2,3,4,4,5,5,6,7,8,8,9,10 (8 hits ≤6), cleaning up their fleet exactly and subduing the planet

stages.heading: Stardate 2411.0
commentary: We can enjoy one last reprieve before the final DM spawns on the board. We build 3 more Cruisers (-36). The ion storm rolls a 7 for movement, staying stationary.

stages.heading: Stardate 2411.1D (The Battle for Abydos)
commentary: In one essence, it could be argued that Abydos still counts as a NPA planet, even though we subdued them. The battle begins, only against their DM!
1. Round 1: Their DM rolled a 4 for weakness (minelayers). It then rolled 1,1,1,6,6,10 against our cruisers (5 hits ≤7)
• Our 12 ships rolled 2/3,3,4,4,4,5,5,6,6,8,10 (3 hits ≤3), reducing the DM's HP to 6
• Round 2: Their DM rolled 1,3,3,4,5,10 against our cruisers (5 hits ≤7)
• Our 9 ships rolled 9/1,1,4,4,7,7,7,10 (2 hits ≤3), reducing the DM's HP to 4
• Round 3: Their DM rolled 2,3,4,6,9,10 against our cruisers (4 hits ≤7)
• Our 7 ships rolled 10/3,6,8,8,9,9 (1 hit ≤3), reducing the DM's HP to 3
• Round 4: Their DM rolled 1,3,5,6,8,9 against our cruisers (4 hits ≤7)
• Our 5 ships rolled 2/1,2,3,4 (4 hits ≤3), finally cleaning up this DM

stages.heading: Stardate 2411.1P
commentary: Yet another victory under our belt, but our fleet sustained heavy losses...

stages.heading: Stardate 2411.4
commentary: Another pirate... Since our numbers are low, we will hire this pirate. Even if we did not already have the 10 CP on hand, it simply counts against our income in the next economic phase.

stages.heading: Stardate 2411.7
commentary: We have found a lot of stuff in the south-western stretch of deep space, thanks to a regional map.

stages.heading: Stardate 2412.0
commentary: The clock has finally ran out. The final DM is now on the board. We rolled 5,3, spawning an extermination DM in the center.
Since we hired the pirate (-10), this reduces our money available for our own ships. We are still able to buy 4 Cruisers (-48).
Finally, the ion storm rolled a 2. It tried to move, but our planet blocked its pathway. (And besides, cosmic storms do not enter home systems.)

stages.heading: Stardate 2412.3
commentary: Taking the unnecessary battles directive into consideration, that leaves the final DM with only a single path...

stages.heading: Stardate 2412.6
commentary: ... one that does not uncover any new unexplored systems.

stages.heading: Stardate 2412.9
commentary: Still, the previous battle put quite a damper in our ability to, well, battle; and we have neither invested in Movement tech, nor have we built a pipeline network.

stages.heading: Stardate 2413.0
commentary: Not all hope is yet lost though. We invest in Ship Size 4 (-20), for what little it matters. We can also use the Ship Yard that was previously built to upgrade our Flagship, and use it to build an extra Cruiser (-12) and an extra Ship Yard (-6).
The ion storm rolls a 3. It tried to move, but our home systems are off limits.

stages.heading: Stardate 2413.2
commentary: The final battle looms ever closer...

stages.heading: Stardate 2413.5
commentary: The fate of the known universe rests on the survival of the Galactic Capitol.

stages.heading: Stardate 2413.8 (The Battle for the Known Universe)
commentary: The final DM is finally here! Whoever wins this battle wins this scenario!
1. Round 1: The DM rolls a 4 for weakness (minelayers)
• The DM rolls a 9 against our pirate (hit ≤9), followed by 1,2,3,6,10 against our cruisers (4 hits ≤8)
• Our 15 ships rolled 9/1,1,1,3,3,3,4,4,4,5,7,8,8,9 (3 hits ≤2), reducing the DM's HP to 7
• Round 2: The DM rolls 2,2,5,6,7,9 against our cruisers (5 hits ≤8)
• Our 13 ships rolled 4/1,3,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,10 (1 hit ≤2), reducing the DM's HP to 6
• Round 3: The DM rolls 1,1,2,4,4,10 against our cruisers (5 hits ≤8)
• Our 10 ships rolled 5/2,2,4,7,8,9,10,10,10 (2 hits ≤2), reducing the DM's HP to 4
• Round 4: The DM rolls 2,2,2,3,5,7 against our cruisers (6 hits ≤8)
• Our 7 ships rolled 8/1,3,9,9,10,10 (1 hit ≤2), reducing the DM's HP to 3
• Round 5: The DM rolls 1,2,4,6,7,10 against our cruisers (5 hits ≤8)
• Our 5 ships rolled 8/1,3,6,10 (1 hit ≤2), reducing the DM's HP to 2
• Round 6: The DM rolls 3,7,9,9,9,10 against our cruisers (2 hits ≤8)
• Our 4 ships rolled 7/2,7,9 (1 hit ≤2), reducing the DM's HP to 1
• Round 7: The DM rolls 8,3,4,9,7,5 against our cruisers (5 hits ≤8), cleanly eliminating them
• Our Flagship, now all alone, rolled a 7 (≤2 to hit)
• Round 8: The DM rolls 1,2,2,3,10,10 (4 hits ≤8); eliminating our flagship and, therefore, the last of the galactic capitol's protection

stages.heading: Stardate 2414.0 (Game Over)
commentary: With the Galactic Capitol eliminated, the fate of the universe is doomed... for eternity. Such is an unfortunate side effect of trying our hand at our own designs, only to see it backfire.

A series of calcs using the Battle Simulator suggests we still had a good chance of winning (roughly 76.69% experimental probability after 12,400 runs). Bad dice rolls can and do happen in any playthrough, as has happened here when the DM survived its battle with 1 HP.
Since our first attempt at this layout ended in defeat, that already concludes this campaign. No sense in ramping it up if we did not successfully win the first difficulty.
